senses.definition: A rare or specialized term referring to a group or arrangement of seven units, typically in a historical or scholarly context.
examples: The manuscript describes a septemfid layout used by ancient scholars.
In the taxonomy, a septemfid class is noted for seven-part divisions.

senses.definition: Having seven streams or seven flowing elements; used in rare or technical descriptors.
examples: The septemfluous pattern appears in the decorative knot.
A septemfluous river network is described in the atlas.

senses.definition: Having seven leaves or leaf-like divisions; often used in botanical descriptions.
examples: A septemfoliate leaf pattern is rare but documented.
The septemfoliate arrangement aids in photosynthesis efficiency.

senses.definition: Having seven leaflets or seven foliolate parts.
examples: The septemfoliolate leaf is depicted in the plant key.
Septemfoliolate leaves radiated from the central stem.

senses.definition: A historical or obsolete term referring to an infection of the bloodstream; today typically called septicemia or sepsis.
examples: Septemia was once used to describe bloodstream infection before modern terminology.
Symptoms of septemia were recorded in early medical texts.

senses.definition: Divided into seven parts or segments.
examples: A septempartite partition was used in the ancient floor plan.
The document describes a septempartite division of duties.

senses.definition: Having seven layers or folds; used in technical descriptions.
examples: The septemplicate skin shows seven distinct layers.
In the model, septemplicate coatings provide insulation.

senses.definition: Containing or full of seven pathways or channels; used in descriptive, often technical, language.
examples: The septemvious network was mapped in the study.
A septemvious sieve allows seven channels to pass through.

senses.definition: One of a group of seven men appointed to administer a state, especially in ancient Rome.
examples: The septemviri were chosen to govern temporarily.
Each septemvir held specific duties within the council.

senses.definition: Relating to a group of seven officials or to the septemviri.
examples: Septemviral authority was limited by the constitution.
Documents describe septemviral decrees.

senses.definition: A group or panel of seven officials collectively; the office or tenure of such a group.
examples: The septemvirate controlled the province for a decade.
Scholars study the political dynamics of the septemvirate.

senses.definition: Multiple instances of a septemvirate; several seven-official governing bodies.
examples: Various periods saw different septemvirates in power.
Historical records mention several septemvirates.

senses.definition: Members of a septemvirate; seven officials collectively.
examples: The septemviri convened to discuss the policy.
Each of the septemviri contributed a report.

senses.definition: The seven members of a septemvirate; seven officials acting together.
examples: The septemvirs issued a joint statement.
The influence of the septemvirs waned over time.

senses.definition: A unit or group of seven; used in various specialized fields.
examples: A septenar division was noted in the chart.
The septenar tariff applied to seven categories.

senses.definition: A person who is seventy years old; a septuagenarian.
examples: The event honored septenarians in the community.
A septenarian shared stories from seven decades.

senses.definition: Groups or collections of seven; plural form of septenary.
examples: The manuscript lists septenaries of verses.
Septenaries organize the text into seven-part cycles.

senses.definition: A Latin plural term referring to seven-part groups or units; used in historical or classical contexts.
examples: The septenarii fragments were studied by philologists.
Septenarii divisions appear in the codex.

senses.definition: A Latin term meaning a group of seven; used in academic or classical references.
examples: The poet cited a septenarius structure.
Septenarius lines were analyzed for meter.

senses.definition: A group or set of seven; a sevenfold division.
examples: The calendar had a septenary week in some traditions.
A septenary pattern appears in the ritual cycle.

senses.definition: To divide into seven parts.
examples: The plan was septenated into seven tasks.
They septenate the resources among seven teams.

senses.definition: Occurring every seventeen years; relating to a seventeen-year period.
examples: The festival is septendecennial, happening once every seventeen years.
Septendecennial reviews occur in rare governance cycles.

senses.definition: A very large number equal to 10^57 in the American system or 10^66 in the long scale; specifically 1 followed by 57 or 66 zeros depending on scale.
examples: A septendecillion is far larger than everyday quantities.
In the debate about big numbers, septendecillion is often cited as an extreme example.

senses.definition: Plural form of septendecillion; extremely large numbers.
examples: Theoretical tables included septendecillions of possibilities.
Comparison of data sets reached septendecillions in scale.

senses.definition: The ordinal form of septendecillion; the position after septendecillions in a sequence.
examples: He calculated to the septendecillionth place, which is impractically small.
The measurement was accurate to the septendecillionth of a unit.
